defaultTab.description: Default page when entering a chat conversation

autoLaunch.openAtLoginDesc: Automatically start ChatLab when the system boots

toolsPanel.positionDesc: Choose where the quick tools panel appears

network.title: Network
network.proxyModeDesc: How to connect when accessing external services
network.invalidProxyUrl: Please enter a valid proxy address, e.g. http://127.0.0.1:7890
network.onlyHttpSupported: Only http:// or https:// protocols are supported

defaultModel.title: Default Model
defaultModel.noConfigs: Please add a model configuration in "Model Service" first

assistantModel.label: Default Assistant Model
assistantModel.description: Used for AI chat, tool calls, SQL assistant, and context compression

fastModel.label: Fast Model
fastModel.description: Used for session summary generation. Falls back to the default assistant model if not set

empty.title: No AI service configured
empty.description: Add a configuration to start using AI features

modal.configNamePlaceholderPreset: Leave empty to use provider name
modal.configNamePlaceholderCustom: Leave empty to use API endpoint
modal.apiKeyPlaceholderEdit: Enter new API Key (leave empty to keep current)
modal.apiKeyPlaceholderLocal: Usually not required for local services
modal.apiKeyHintLocal: Enter if the service requires authentication
modal.saveAnywayHint: Validation did not pass. The configuration may be incorrect. Save anyway?
modal.apiEndpointOverrideHint: Default URL is prefilled. Replace if needed
modal.customModelIdHint: The model identifier used in API calls
modal.contextWindowHint: Maximum context tokens supported by this model (used for context compression)
modal.contextWindowMissing: Context window not set, will use default. Click "Edit Model" to set it manually
modal.modelRequired: Add at least one model to save

storage.title: Local Storage Management
storage.description: Manage data files stored locally by ChatLab

dataLocation.title: Data Directory
dataLocation.description: Customize where chat databases and vector indexes are stored
dataLocation.managedDescription: This setting only manages chat databases and vector indexes. AI chats, cache, and logs remain in the fixed system data directory.
dataLocation.serverPathHint: Enter an absolute path that is accessible from the machine running the CLI server.
dataLocation.migrateHint: Chat database data is still at a legacy location. Migrate to the unified directory:
dataLocation.restartTip: Please restart the app after switching. Chat database data will be migrated based on your choice.
dataLocation.cliRestartTip: Restart the CLI server after switching. Chat database data will be migrated before the server starts.
dataLocation.confirmMessage: You are about to change the chat database directory. If migration is selected, existing chat database data will be copied to the new directory.
dataLocation.confirmWarning: After restarting, the old chat database directory may be automatically deleted. Please ensure the new path is correct.
dataLocation.cliConfirmWarning: After restarting the CLI server, chat database data will be migrated to the new location. Make sure the path is correct and writable by the server process.
dataLocation.pathRequired: Please enter a data directory path
dataLocation.installDirForbidden: Data directory cannot be placed inside the app installation directory, as it will be cleared during updates
dataLocation.migrationSuccessMessage: The app needs to restart for changes to take effect.
dataLocation.cliMigrationSuccessMessage: Stop the current CLI server and start it again. Migration will run before the server starts. Reopen the page afterward to see the new directory.

ai.name: AI Chat Databases
ai.description: AI chat history and config files

statsCache.name: Cache Data
statsCache.description: Statistics cache for chat records, auto-rebuilt after clearing

logs.name: Log Files
logs.description: App logs including import, AI, error logs

session.title: Session Index Settings
session.description: Session index splits chat history into conversation segments by time gaps for better AI analysis
session.thresholdHelp: Messages exceeding this time gap will be split into a new session
session.batchRegenerateConfirmMessage: This will clear all existing AI summaries and regenerate session indexes from scratch.
session.batchRegenerateConfirmWarning: Existing summaries will be overwritten. This action cannot be undone.
session.summaryStrategyHelp: Controls how detailed AI-generated summaries will be

semanticIndex.title: Semantic Index
semanticIndex.description: Builds an index of your chats with the selected vector model. Once enabled, the AI can semantically retrieve relevant past excerpts as supporting evidence.
semanticIndex.mirrorSourceHint: HF-Mirror is a third-party public mirror for networks in mainland China. Model files will be downloaded from hf-mirror.com.
semanticIndex.localDownloadHint: The model downloads automatically after saving. The first download may take a while.
semanticIndex.apiKeyHint: API keys are stored separately and securely; the config keeps only a reference.
semanticIndex.apiCostWarning: API mode may incur provider costs. Please confirm model pricing.
semanticIndex.changeRebuildHint: After changing the model or API, enabled conversations need to be rebuilt.
semanticIndex.modelError: Model download failed. It will be retried when building the index.
semanticIndex.modelDownloading: Downloading model, this may take a few minutes on first use…

searchMaxResults.label: Excerpts per search
searchMaxResults.hint: Default number of relevant excerpts returned when the AI runs a semantic search (5-15). The AI may raise it as needed.

semanticIndex.cleanupConfirm: This deletes index data from disabled conversations and old models. It does not delete chat records.

session.enableHint: Once enabled, the AI can retrieve past chat excerpts when answering questions about this conversation.
session.configFirst: Configure a vector model under Settings > AI > Semantic Index before enabling.
session.buildVsEnable: Enable Index: include this conversation in semantic retrieval. Build Index: read all messages and generate vectors, which may use local compute or API quota.

picker.title: Select Conversations
picker.confirmRemoveMessage: The semantic index for these conversations will be permanently deleted.

chatPreferences.title: Conversation Preferences
chatPreferences.enableAutoSkillDesc: When enabled, AI will automatically match and activate suitable analysis skills based on user queries; when disabled, skills only take effect when manually selected

chartAutoMode.title: Auto Chart Generation
chartAutoMode.description: Controls how actively AI uses charts when the Chart Assistant is not manually selected
chartAutoMode.explicitHint: AI only generates charts when you explicitly ask for them.
chartAutoMode.suggestHint: Conservative strategy: AI only generates charts when they clearly help explain the analysis.
chartAutoMode.aggressiveHint: AI more actively adds charts in data analysis scenarios.

maxMessages.title: Max Messages per Request
maxMessages.description: Max number of messages sent to AI per request. Higher values consume more tokens but provide more accurate context (recommended: 2000).

searchContext.title: Search Context Window
searchContext.description: Automatically include surrounding messages for each search hit to help AI understand the context. Set to 0 to disable

exportFormat.title: Export Format
exportFormat.description: File format for exporting AI conversations

sqlExportFormat.title: SQL Lab Export Format
sqlExportFormat.description: File format for exporting SQL query results

compression.title: Context Compression
compression.enableDesc: Automatically compress long conversation history into summaries to reduce token usage
compression.thresholdDesc: Trigger compression when context tokens reach this percentage of the model's context window
compression.bufferDesc: Percentage of recent messages to keep uncompressed
compression.maxToolResultPercentDesc: Maximum data percentage per tool call sent to AI (based on model context window). Excess results are intelligently truncated

about.title: About ChatLab
about.webUpdateHint: Run clb update in your terminal, then restart the service.

privacy.title: Privacy Settings
privacy.analyticsDesc: When enabled, the app collects non-sensitive data like version number and OS version to help improve the product

developer.title: Developer Options
developer.debugModeDesc: When enabled, the debug panel and quick debugging tools will be shown. You can use them to view databases, run testing tools, and record more detailed debug logs.

aiPreprocess.title: Chat Preprocessing
aiPreprocess.description: Preprocess chat records before sending to AI to save tokens and filter noise. Original data is not modified.
aiPreprocess.dataCleaningDesc: Automatically clean XML card messages (shared links, mini-programs, etc.), extracting useful content and significantly saving tokens. Recommended to keep enabled.
aiPreprocess.mergeConsecutiveDesc: Merge consecutive messages from the same sender within a short time window
aiPreprocess.denoiseDesc: Filter out meaningless messages like interjections, emojis, and system placeholders (e.g. [Image])
aiPreprocess.desensitizeDesc: Replace sensitive information (phone numbers, emails, ID numbers, etc.) based on rules
aiPreprocess.anonymizeNamesDesc: Replace real nicknames with IDs (U1, U2…) when sending to AI, reducing hallucinations and saving tokens
aiPreprocess.blacklistDesc: Messages containing any of these keywords will be filtered out entirely
aiPreprocess.blacklistPlaceholder: Type a keyword and press Enter to add

groups.credentials_desc: Credentials such as API keys and Bearer tokens
groups.global_contact_desc: Cross-region contact details such as email addresses
groups.global_financial_desc: Financial identifiers such as credit card numbers
groups.global_network_desc: Network identifiers such as URLs and IP addresses
groups.region_cn_desc: Rules for phone numbers, IDs, and bank cards in Mainland China
groups.region_us_desc: Rules for phone numbers and IDs in the United States
groups.region_jp_desc: Rules for phone numbers and My Number in Japan
groups.region_kr_desc: Rules for phone numbers and resident registration numbers in Korea

rules.api_key_prefix_desc: Strings starting with sk-, ghp_, AKIA, etc.
rules.url_desc: Links starting with http:// or https://

service.title: API Service
service.enableDesc: When enabled, external tools can query, export, and import chat data via HTTP API. Localhost only (127.0.0.1)
service.portInUseHint: Port is in use. Please change the port below or close the program using this port

port.title: Port Configuration
port.label: Listen Port
port.desc: Local port for the API service (1024-65535)

token.title: Access Token
token.label: Bearer Token
token.desc: All API requests must include this token in the Authorization header
token.noToken: No token generated yet. Please enable the service first

dataSources.title: Data Sources
dataSources.desc: Configure external data source URLs. ChatLab will automatically pull and import chat data at the specified interval.
dataSources.noSessions: No import sessions. Click "Manage Sessions" above to add.

form.name: Name
form.tokenPlaceholder: Bearer Token for the remote API

discovery.error: Failed to connect to remote server
discovery.connectionCheckFailed: Cannot connect to the remote server. Please check the URL and token.

deleteConfirm.title: Delete Data Source
deleteConfirm.message: Are you sure you want to delete "{name}"? All import configurations under this data source will be removed. Already imported local conversations will not be deleted.

deleteSessionConfirm.title: Delete Subscription
deleteSessionConfirm.message: Are you sure you want to delete "{name}"?

usage.title: Usage Guide
usage.desc: After enabling the service, external tools can query data or push chat records to ChatLab via API. All requests require Bearer Token authentication.

overview.title: MCP Overview
overview.description: MCP lets AI assistants directly query your chat records. Supports session discovery, keyword search, member stats, time distribution, and more.
overview.installHint: Install chatlab-cli, then add the config below to your AI client:

config.title: Client Configuration
config.claudeHint: Add this config to ClaudeCode's MCP configuration.
config.codexHint: Add this TOML config to your ~/.codex/config.toml file.
config.openclawHint: Add this config to your project's openclaw.json file.
config.otherHint: Add this JSON config to your MCP client configuration file.

tips.title: Tips
tips.dataDir: The MCP server reads chat data from ~/.chatlab/, sharing the same data as the desktop app.
tips.readOnly: All operations are read-only and will not modify your chat records.
tips.nodeRequired: npx and CLI modes require Node.js 20 or higher.

lock.title: App Lock
lock.disableConfirm: Disable app lock? Your current PIN will be deleted.

password.errorSameAsOld: New PIN must differ from current PIN
